programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps an old friend recruit a new generation of volunteers in an effort to save community cats in Philadelphia

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Gerry investigates a situation involving stray bullets while checking teal hunters along the border fence. Turtle hatchlings are released on South Padre Island.

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil seeks help from McCarthys unlikeliest resident as he pursues his mine tour project, and Tim gets a reminder of home when Mark pays him a surprise visit

programme.title: Deadly Islands
programme.desc: Biologist and predator specialist Dave Salmoni journeys to an island in the South Pacific to investigate why its waters are home to a large concentration of sharks

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.desc: Jeff is pleased with Bob and Screech learning how to feed themselves. He also teaches them how to den up for the winter - and the bears get territorial when Jeffs friend arrives

programme.title: Richard E Grants Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: Richard E Grant explores the evolutionary roots of greed - the act of taking too much. From hamsters, to baboons, do animals also display this seemingly human trait?

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his team are challenged with creating a lighthouse treehouse that sits atop a seawall along the coast of Washington, but face a logistical nightmare along the way

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: Members of the team rescue a cat trapped in a drain deep underground, and help an abandoned dog in danger of freezing to death

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack recalls the golden age of shipbuilding, examining the skills of engineers who turned Britain into one of the leading manufacturing nations, and visits Bristol to pay tribute to his personal hero Isambard Kingdom Brunel. He also travels to Scotland for a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley

programme.title: Building Britains Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team creates an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: Catching Historys Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victims skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack recalls the golden age of shipbuilding, examining the skills of engineers who turned Britain into one of the leading manufacturing nations, and visits Bristol to pay tribute to his personal hero Isambard Kingdom Brunel. He also travels to Scotland for a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the changing fortunes of a family of architects, following the rise and fall of George Gilbert Scott, his son George Junior, and grandson Giles. Presenter Dan Cruickshank finds out how the dynasty was responsible for the creation of iconic landmarks such as the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station, and helped shape the Victorian age with their designs

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)
programme.desc: A laconic gunslinger, a ruthless hired killer and a conniving outlaw hunt for a cache of loot during the chaos of the American Civil War. However, each is privy to only part of the information necessary to find it, setting in motion a series of deadly betrayals. The third film in Sergio Leones Spaghetti Western trilogy, starring Clint Eastwood as the Man with No Name, with Lee Van Cleef and Eli Wallach. Featuring Ennio Morricones iconic soundtrack

programme.title: Clint Eastwood: The Man with No Name
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor and film director in which he talks to Iain Johnstone, with Richard Burton, Sergio Leone, Don Siegel, Pauline Kael, Dilys Powell. There is extracts from some of Eastwoods films, including Rawhide, The Good, the Bad and the Ugly, Where Eagles Dare, The Outlaw Josey Wales and The Enforcer. First broadcast 23 February 1977

programme.title: Word Up! Black American Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of BBC performances by African-American artists of the 1980s, featuring Salt-N-Pepa, Cameo, Luther Vandross, Doug E Fresh, Whitney Houston, Chaka Khan, Shalamar and Kid Creole

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain

Presenter Dan Cruickshank finds out how the dynasty was responsible for the creation of iconic landmarks such as the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station, and helped shape the Victorian age with their designs

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast September 10, 1999. Including performances from Shaft, Enrique Iglesias, Shania Twain, The Offspring, and Lou Bega

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Emma Ledden presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast September 17, 1999. Featuring performances from Sixpence None The Richer, Thunderbugs, The All-seeing I Feat Phil Oakey, Lyte Funky Ones, and Vengaboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Richard Skinner and Janice Long introduce performances by Culture Club, the Human League, FR David, Twisted Sister, Toto, Heaven 17, Thompson Twins, Spandau Ballet and David Bowie

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the chart show from April 21, 1994, featuring Crash Test Dummies, Bitty McLean, CJ Lewis, the Pretenders, Erasure, Crystal Waters, Deacon Blue, Toni Di Bart and Prince

programme.title: Prince and His Songs at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive appearances on the BBC by the musician as well as performances by artists who covered his songs over the years, including Beyonce, Muse, Tom Jones, the Bangles, Pink, Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka, Chaka Khan and Sinead OConnor

programme.title: Prince and the Revolution: Live
programme.desc: A 1985 performance by the American musician and his band from the Purple Rain tour at the Syracuse Carrier Dome in New York, remixed, remastered and visually enhanced. The film features hits from the popular albums Purple Rain, Controversy and 1999, including an 18-minute version of Purple Rain

programme.title: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: Profile of the American musician, who rose to fame in the 1980s with hits including 1999, Kiss and Raspberry Beret. As well as achieving significant commercial success around the world, he won critical acclaim for his adventurous, genre-blurring albums, such as Sign O the Times and Around the World in a Day - while also making headlines for his sexually explicit lyrics and stage shows, as well as his legal battle to retain control of his name and music. Featuring contributions by guitarist Dez Dickerson, Public Enemy frontman Chuck D, soul singer Beverley Knight and Paisley Park label president Alan Leeds

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: RAF Saves Trapped Cow and Police Catch Poaching Suspect
programme.desc: Police uncover shocking evidence of animal cruelty linked to a series of farmland poaching attacks in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, and the RAF tackle a challenging rescue when Beauty the cow becomes trapped at the bottom of a steep gorge in Gilsland, Cumbria. Paul Colgan joins a rural crime officer using forensic DNA technology to mark valuable machinery in Ellerton, East Yorkshire

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has to navigate his way through an eight-bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui Joseph takes in the views in Dover, and Tommy Walsh looks around a bungalow in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a special show, Eric Knowles celebrates the glamour and sophistication of the 1920s and 30s. He looks back at a host of art deco-inspired pieces bought by Bargain Hunt teams, from cocktail sets and mirrors to Anglepoise lamps and bronze sculptures. Eric also admires the geometric architecture of the Jazz Age and visits Stockports stunning art deco cinema, as well as a unique home in Northampton designed by Charles Rennie Mackintosh

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Llandudno, competitors Anne and Lenka race to turn charity shop bargains into profit, taking bold risks on furniture, jewellery and creative upcycles, in the hope of raising money for Cancer Research Wales. Presented by Joanna Page

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo Beale makes over two spaces on the Isle of Mull, turning a Tobermory castle turret room into a BandB destination and revamping a guest bedroom at the Rubha nan Gall lighthouse. Contending with complicated logistics and bad weather, Banjo and the team must go above and beyond to complete two makeovers in these stunning locations

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps a couple with four kids relocate to North Yorkshire, and meets a couple who have turned their passion for bee-keeping into a buzzing new life

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits ceramic sculptor Sarah from Yorkshire and glass artist Emmy from Devon to see how their fortunes have changed. When Sarah arrived at Doms bootcamp, she was new to her craft, but thanks to his support and guidance, she has built a growing business. Last time, Emmy was returning to glassblowing after an eight-year break. Now that she is selling around the world

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Graeme, Anjy, Daniel, Mike and Emily race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Tom Allen host the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing from bustling Istanbu to the remote town of Halfeti in south-east Turkey - where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition. Along the way, Katie persuades brother Harrison to tick off a bucket list experience - watching the hot-air balloons rise over the chimneys of Cappadocia - while the leaders in the last leg, cousins Puja and Roshni, continue to celebrate, this time because its Pujas birthday

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its the final and as usual the remaining two candidates go head to head to win Lord Sugars investment. Supported by a handful of returning candidates, they must launch their businesses to a roomful of industry and a few familiar faces from the past 20 years, before the business mogul decides who will become his next partner

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and JB Gill present the magazine-style programme with usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: Unza Saleem is given the task of painting brothers Jordan and Cian, who carry a faulty gene that means they will almost certainly develop symptoms of dementia in their forties. However, just as Unza is getting to grips with the portrait, she has her own family emergency that threatens to derail the project entirely

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigates when a seaweed farm is targeted in an act of sabotage, with fisherman Jeff Simmons swearing that he saw the legendary mermaid of Shipton Abbott on the night of the incident. With no logical explanation for how someone could damage the farm and get away unseen among glowing bioluminescent waters, Esther braces herself for an excited Humphrey to run wild with the possibility that their prime suspect could be a mermaid

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sports presenter Gabby Logan hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedians Miles Jupp and Michelle Wolf joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conors one true love Linda Walsh is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor Phil Dunster, who rose to fame in hit show Ted Lasso, to talk about his new comedy Rooster. Actress Cush Jumbo chats about the second season of crime thriller Criminal Record, and comedian Josh Widdicombe discusses his podcast The Museum of Pop Culture, while Dan Levy, creator, writer and star of the multi-award winning show Schitts Creek, discusses the series

programme.title: The Royal Hotel (2023)
programme.desc: American best friends Hanna and Liv run out of money in Sydney as they backpack around Australia. The cash-strapped twentysomethings seek temporary employment to pay for the rest of the trip. However, after taking a job in a pub, they are confronted by the unruly locals. Thriller, starring Julia Garner, Jessica Henwick, Hugo Weaving and Ursula Yovich

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the Time Lords adventures in the 60th anniversary specials

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond debate which company has made the greatest number of exceptional vehicles, and James May finds a way of transporting caravans to campsites without inconveniencing other road users. The Top Gear track plays host to a replica of one of the worlds most famous rally cars, and broadcaster Chris Evans is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: When a teenage boy has a girl over for the first time, the overly attached monster under his bed has a meltdown. Comedy short film, starring Freddie Meredith

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steves daughter. If they fail, Steves in the doghouse and Stu might end up dead. Comic short film, written by and starring Stuart McPherson and Stephen Buchanan

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated comedy set in 1990s east London. Joyces birthday present has been stolen, so her husband enlists the help of his fearsome mother-in-law to track it down

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, where the cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical and Gambo forgets how to breathe

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Donna Nobles past catches up with her, as she finds herself reunited with the time traveller. The first of three editions originally broadcast for the shows 60th anniversary, starring David Tennant as the 14th Doctor and Catherine Tate as companion Donna Noble

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An out-of-control Tardis takes the Doctor and Donna to the furthest edge of adventure, to face the most desperate fight of their lives, with the fate of the universe at stake

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The giggle of a mysterious puppet is driving the human race insane. When the Doctor discovers the return of an old enemy, he faces a fight he can never win. Neil Patrick Harris guest stars as 1960s villain the Toymaker and Ncuti Gatwa makes his debut as the 15th Doctor

programme.title: Talking Doctor Who
programme.desc: David Tennant time travels back through the BBC archive to tell the story of the Doctors classic era, in a programme originally shown as part of the 60th anniversary celebrations. The selection features rarely seen interviews and clips covering William Hartnells first incarnation right through to the seventh Doctor, Sylvester McCoy, and David compares his experiences with those who came before him

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the first of Doctor Whos 60th anniversary special episodes

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the second in the series of specials marking the 60th anniversary of the sci-fi series

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer visits some of the worlds most dangerous cities to explore the issues that affect young men born into a life on the fringes of society. He begins in the favelas of Rio, Brazil, where he learns what it takes to become a lookout, foot soldier and drug dealer in a world where death could become reality at any moment. He also comes face-to-face with a gun-toting militia boss to uncover how these men rule through fear and intimidation

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Talking Doctor Who
programme.desc: David Tennant time travels back through the BBC archive to tell the story of the Doctors classic era, in a programme originally shown as part of the 60th anniversary celebrations. The selection features rarely seen interviews and clips covering William Hartnells first incarnation right through to the seventh Doctor, Sylvester McCoy, and David compares his experiences with those who came before him

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Dance Race
programme.desc: New Zealand choreographer Corey Bakers short film which a range of dancers, from ballet and Strictly stars to choreographed skydivers and drivers, race across the UK to meet Lady G

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores habitats shared by humans and dangerous creatures, and helps remove a venomous snake from the garden of a family in South America

programme.title: Puss in Boots (2011)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giants castle. He must save the day when it transpires that their actions have unleashed a terrible danger on his home town. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A famous chef is poisoned at the grand opening of his latest hotel, during a cooking competition in which various hopefuls compete for the head chef position. The team are left confounded when they realise that the victim didnt consume anything that day that all the contestants hadnt also consumed themselves. Catherine finds herself entangled in the case when an old friend becomes a murder suspect

programme.title: Legend (2015)
programme.desc: Biopic of the Kray brothers, exploring their rise to power in the London underworld and conflicts with rival gangs from the point of view of Reggies wife Frances, who remains devoted to her husband even as he remains equally bound to his brutal, unstable sibling. Starring Tom Hardy as both twins, with Emily Browning, Christopher Eccleston and David Thewlis

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is offered a job on a cruise ship but realises taking it will mean leaving Jonny and Corinthian behind. Louise learns the truth about her parents and Donna goes into business

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donnas career falters just as Janets is taking off. Louise gets a letter from her mother but cant face reading it, and proud father Jonny leads the charge in the fight for baby-changing facilities in the pub

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Donna Nobles past catches up with her, as she finds herself reunited with the time traveller. The first of three editions originally broadcast for the shows 60th anniversary, starring David Tennant as the 14th Doctor and Catherine Tate as companion Donna Noble

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An out-of-control Tardis takes the Doctor and Donna to the furthest edge of adventure, to face the most desperate fight of their lives, with the fate of the universe at stake

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The giggle of a mysterious puppet is driving the human race insane. When the Doctor discovers the return of an old enemy, he faces a fight he can never win. Neil Patrick Harris guest stars as 1960s villain the Toymaker and Ncuti Gatwa makes his debut as the 15th Doctor

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: South West England: Main and Dessert
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from south west England cook mains and desserts and creations are judged by veteran judge Simon Rogan. They include a guinea fowl dish designed to bring Aardman character Feathers McGraw to life, as well as a rum-soaked savarin, earl grey tea mousse and a milk ice cream paying homage to Sense and Sensibility

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: When the team attend a Dark Morris Dancing event, Margo finds herself at the centre of a crime when a member of her team collapses during their performance. With a severe allergic reaction to blame, and the victims epi-pen case empty, Humphrey is stumped as to how the attack was triggered unseen while all eyes were on the stage. Death in Paradise spin-off, starring Kris Marshall and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Diversitys Ashley Banjo, actresses Hermione Norris and Patsy Kensit., reality star Tasha Ghouri; stand-up comedians Hasan Al-Habib and Ashley Blaker and broadcaster Jayne Middlemiss make a journey through the landscapes of north-east England to Lindisfarne, one of Britains most important pilgrimage sites. They trek through the heart of Northumberland and the Scottish Borders along a curated network of trails celebrating three of Englands most revered seventh-century northern English saints - Hild, Oswald and Cuthbert

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell is struck by the fetching colour of a County Durham house, while Dion Dublin is impressed by whats on offer in Greater Manchester and Derbyshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who have never lived in an older home, but who are now ready to fall for a bit of period charm in either Norfolk or Suffolk. With a £270,000 budget and three lively whippets to please, they are after a dog-friendly garden and a slice of East Anglian architectural history to call home. Alistair also meets a willow farmer and discovers how this wonderfully sustainable crop is being woven into the future

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Frances Tophill returns to Damson Farm, and shares research from the RSPB on how to support garden birds. She also reveals how to make the most of sowing older seeds, and gets hands-on preparing vibrant container displays ready for the sunny days ahead. Toby Buckland explains how to make the most of outdoor spaces. And Joe Swift travels to the Cornish coast to meet a gardener who proves that even the wildest seaside weather cant stop a true planting paradise

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: On the Western tip of Jamaica Clive meets up with cousins he hasnt seen in decades. In the kitchen, he serves up a classic island dish of curry goat for all the guests. Clive also learns what it takes to harvest Jamaican honey, tries his hand at some unique pottery and makes an emotional return to the primary school his mother taught in before she emigrated to Britain in 1962

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Michael Scott uses 3D scanning technology to reveal the historical secrets of ancient Athens and tell the story of how this remarkable city created the worlds first democracy. He begins his journey on the Acropolis, where, in the late sixth century BC the people of Athens overthrew a tyrant and set up a democracy, and investigates a mysterious, asymmetrical temple called the Erechtheion that sits in the shadow of the world-famous Parthenon. To the south of the city, he clambers through a silver mine where an army of slaves once toiled inside the labyrinth of narrow tunnels

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents a programme from the Victoria and Albert Museum, where the team of antiques experts value treasures including letters written by Lewis Carroll, a collection of Japanese hair decorations, and a necklace with beads worn by Egyptian mummies 4,000 years ago

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the locations from the series, including Greenwich Royal Naval College, Althorp House in Northamptonshire, and Herstmonceux Castle, East Sussex

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo starts a journey in the coastal city of Kanazawa, where he goes behind the scenes with the cleaning detail. At Kanazawa Castle, Michael discovers the history of one of the most important and powerful samurai clans in Japanese history. To finish the day, Michael travels to the Kurobe Gorge railway, a narrow gauge mountain line which snakes along one of Japans steepest gorges

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West England competition draws to an end, with the final judging, where the two remaining contestants must prepare dishes celebrating the British film industry. Actor, comedian and Wallace and Gromit voice artist Ben Whtehead joins judges Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, and Phil Wang to decide who will be going through to the final

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: King Charles visits Campbeltowns farmers market, where Andrew and Gayle hope to impress with their premium plants. Emma and Rob host a family wedding on their farm, but their pregnant cows could go into labour at any moment, while Callum and Zara hold a historic sheepdog trial attracting competitors from afar

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: Part two of two. Long-term addict Bobby heads to rehab, Billy fights for recognition that he has really changed, while Alan is released and heads back to his old ways on the streets of Glasgow

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon hears from Alan Sugar and winner of the current series. As well as talking to the runner-up, she is also joined by Karren Brady and Tim Campbell

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Nick Watt

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of Paul McCartneys original Hofner bass more than 50 years ago is one of rock n rolls great mysteries and this documentary tells the story of the instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring interviews with the former Beatle, his brother Mike, musicians Klaus Voormann and Elvis Costello, and roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre reports on Donald Trumps hardline immigration policies, which have left an estimated 14 million undocumented immigrants are at risk of deportation and resulted in a sharp rise in arrests in US cities and protesters being shot dead on the streets of Minneapolis by federal agents

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: A group hike up Scafell Pike brings buried emotions and romantic confusion to the surface. During an unforeseen storm, Mary finds herself caught in the middle of rising tensions between Mr Hayward and Mr Ryder. As their expedition takes a turn for the worse, Mary is faced with real danger, and the events of the day leave her feeling more conflicted than ever before

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Charting Michael Jacksons journey from child prodigy to global icon, examining the genius, controversy, trials and tragedy that shaped and continue to divide his legacy. With unique access to friends and family including Jacksons sister, La Toya, this edition charts Michael Jacksons journey from child performer to a deeply troubled musical icon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, where mother and daughter Liz and Alice compete to raise money for Great Ormond Street Hospital. With just £100 each and 60 minutes on the clock, they scour local charity shops, taking a gamble on vintage fashion, ceramics and the bold purchase of a sewing machine

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has to navigate his way through an eight-bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui Joseph takes in the views in Dover, and Tommy Walsh looks around a bungalow in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. Zoe may be a beginner but the science is on her side - getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer heads back to Ireland to reconnect with family and old friends, explore the dramatic western coastline and experience the countrys unique culture, beginning in the county of Cork He meets up with his nephew Ruairí and together they face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. Ronan also forages for edible plants in a rewilding project area and visits the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre on the Beara Peninsula

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Cubas capital, Clive takes on a potential Olympic champion at one of Havanas legendary boxing gyms. He visits a car mechanic to get under the hood of a classic Chevy and discovers why most cars in Cuba are pre-1959 models. Clive also follows in the footsteps of the Buena Vista Social Club to find out how Cuban rhythms conquered the world

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: In Turkey, Michael Scott uses 3D-scanning technology to examine the once enormous Hippodrome, which began as a stadium for chariot racing, though its foundations were later converted into a huge water cistern to supply the Emperors Great Palace. Michael unearths a holy well, hidden 30 feet under a carpet shop and, conversely, walks high above the city on the most sophisticated and longest aqueduct of the Roman world, finding that part of the Great Palace of Constantine is now a burnt-out shell. Along the way Michael finds time for a shave in the Sultans Grande Bazaar and uncovers the engineering innovations of the Suleymaniye Mosque

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents from Nottingham, where the team discovers a pair of outsize boots whose owner could have weighed 52 stone. Other items include memorabilia from the Nuremberg Trials, a shirt worn by an Arsenal player in 1950 and a neo-classical ring found in the spout of a teapot

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Gloucester Cathedral, presenter Paul Martin, along with experts Catherine Southon and Philip Serrell, views some exceptional items brought in for valuation

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: TV presenter Jacqui Joseph, news broadcaster Ben Thompson, weather forecaster Laura Tobin and comedian Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills before the days winner - and the weeks overall victor - is revealed

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ends the first leg of his journey on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps. In the town of Obuse, Michael learns how Japans most famous artwork, Hokusais The Great Wave off Kanagawa, was made. He visits the largest wasabi farm in the world, and heads to the historic mountain town of Narai Juku, with its beautifully preserved wooden houses

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Diarmuid and Carol as they explore three more contenders for Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. They visit a coastal sanctuary in Millisle, a 15-acre woodland and wildlife garden in Ballymoney, and a country cottage with herbaceous borders and a cut flower garden in Greyabbey

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum Clunie demonstrates what can be grown from just one container in a season, and shows what can be produced for a family of four from a medium-sized vegetable garden. Brian Cunningham looks at how to keep lawns looking great and tackles a patio space around Beechgroves chiminea corner, while there is a visit to a new allotment in Perth

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don previews a new garden in Londons Regents Park to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II, due to open on what would have been her 100th birthday. The team also go behind the scenes of the project, with Advolly Richmond uncovering the parks past, from its origins as a Tudor royal hunting ground to its reinvention as wartime allotments, and Adam Frost helping volunteers plant 11,000 spring bulbs

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Conor Spackman is on the trail of Irish home repair scammers crossing America and ripping off millions in cash. The scam starts with a small repair, then escalates into major work that will supposedly save your home - but its really funding a lavish life for the scammers. With the FBI on their case, Conor Spackman follows the money trail and lifts the lid on where the cash goes

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Phil Wang, Brennan Reece, Rachel Parris
programme.desc: Phil Wang comperes the comedy show at Londons Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by star of The Mash Report Rachel Parris and actor, voiceover artist and stand-up Brennan Reece

programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Matt Chorley

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Wicker Man (1973)
programme.desc: A devoutly Christian Scottish police officer travels to a remote island to investigate the disappearance of a missing girl. As his investigations are disrupted at every turn, the detective is horrified to see the population have embraced an ancient pagan lifestyle, and begins to fear they may intend to sacrifice the child in an occult ritual. Horror, starring Edward Woodward, Christopher Lee and Britt Ekland.

programme.desc: Extended highlights of a first-round match from day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister

After a run-in with an old friend, and with Mr Hayward absent from London, Mary wonders what happiness could look like for her. Can she find the courage to finally stand up to her mother and forge a path of her own?

programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.desc: The team investigates a massive bubble anomaly on the ranch. Its members conduct operations to mark the anomalys barrier, leading to shocking discoveries during the process

It is home to mysterious ancient stone circles, stories of strange beings and is a major hotspot for UFO sightings. Given these facts, it has been considered a potential magnet for extraterrestrial visitors

programme.desc: A Vermont woman films a Loch Ness Monster-esque creature in Americas Lake Champlain. Some fear it could be Champ, a legendary plesiosaur-like creature that has been witnessed in the spot over 100 years. Deep in Russia, an enormous explosion is captured on a cars dash cam

programme.desc: Recollections of how a family feud escalated as the Golds revisit a past disagreement between Seth and Les over the direction of the business

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind the Deal
programme.desc: Les, Seth and Ashley recall the deal that nearly broke them and what really happened when Les allowed a $60,000 watch to walk out the door

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team value antiques in Loughborough, Leicestershire. Dealers Simon Schneider and Mark Stevens are put through their paces as they help two teams examine items including a Georg Jensen bowl, a Moorcroft vase and an early fire extinguisher

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team value antiques in Worksop, Nottinghamshire. Dealer Tim Hogarth demonstrates his passion for anything Scottish when a dirk - a type of dagger - is brought to his table. Meanwhile, a clockwork train makes David Ford reminisce about his youth

programme.sub-title: May the Miniforce Be with You

A photographer captures an ominous ring hovering in the sky over downtown Chicago

A construction worker whose finger has been crushed discusses the importance of male role models in his life, and a haemophiliac reflects on living with his condition

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chefs attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: Heatwave (2022)
programme.desc: An ambitious, career-driven young woman seemingly has it all employed in a dynamic job at a real estate agency. However, her life takes a turn when she realises she has fallen for her bosss wife. Secrets are revealed and dark motives linking to her past come to light as she becomes tangled in a web of malice. Thriller, starring Kat Graham, Merritt Patterson, Sebastian Roche and Cardi Wong.

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp shows how to turn trash into treasure with Mexican tin art, soap recycling and a lovable tin can dog

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, George meets some novice DIY renovators who are planning to completely transform a dilapidated Georgian-style crescent house, poorly sub-divided into four flats in the 1980s. With a budget of just £40,000, no building experience and a plan to live onsite with two young children, the stakes could not be higher for this family

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Sam getting his life back on track after several sessions with Frasier, Diane feels it is time to come clean about her relationship with the psychiatrist. American comedy, starring Ted Danson, Shelley Long and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam discovers an intimate secret concerning Dianes romance with her new boyfriend Frasier that leads him to believe she is still madly in love with him, making his ego even bigger

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff meets the woman of his dreams at the Cheers Halloween fancy dress party - but they must work out if they feel the same about each other when the masks come off. John Ratzenberger stars alongside Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to ensure Franks 65th birthday is memorable by buying him an expensive aquarium just like the one he has always admired in a local Chinese restaurant. Unfortunately, one of the fish dies, sinking the Barone household into gloom and discord

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly attends a high-school reunion with Debra, only to discover she had been friends with the in-crowd who made his nerdy teenage years a living hell. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to intercept a strongly worded letter sent by Debra, telling Marie to stop interfering in her affairs. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink is delighted when a station in another city begins relaying his radio show - until he struggles to compete with the popularity of the host he has replaced. Elsewhere, Niles, Daphne and Martins attempt to build a table does not go according to plan

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphnes birthday party sparks a series of unexpected events, and after a night of intimacy, Frasier pursues Roz to her family reunion in Wisconsin, where he pretends to be her estranged partner Roger. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles persuades Daphnes hard-drinking father to call on her mother in Seattle, hoping he can inspire them to start afresh. Meanwhile, Frasier and Roz reflect on their night together. Comedy, guest starring Brian Cox

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Daphne impulsively marry while holidaying in Reno, but on their return they realise Frasier, Martin and Daphnes mother will be distraught if they find out they missed the event. Starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across America saving failing restaurants from disaster. In the first of two visits to Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, the chef encounters Joe Nagy, who claims he has the best menu from New York to Los Angeles. However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The lads head to Birmingham, where they fall out over a bench that Henry thinks has value

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: In Thirsk, the Bakers are given a special tour of James Herriots home by his son Jim, and Matt takes to the skies for a stunning aerial view of the county by glider. Janice meets the towns yarn-bombing guerrilla knitters and visits a stunning lavender farm with Matt. Finally, the Bakers take in what Herriot dubbed the finest view in England, at Sutton Bank

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to Mayenne in France to help engineer Martin and retired gardener Mandy find their dream forever home. After holidaying in the northwest of France many times, they now want to move lock, stock and barrel, with their three motorbikes and two prize parrots! Jean shows them five great properties for their budget of £50,000; will any of them rev Martin and Mandy up enough to make an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: A return to the Algarve to catch up with Cheryl and Graham, who swapped life in Surrey to run a riverside BandB. In France, Devon family Brian and Paula are on the move after closing their popular eatery, while on the Costa del Sol, a turn in fortune may be on the cards for guesthouse owner Louisa

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Lalacelle in Normandy, Terry and Ashley race to finish their opulent 401 suite, but theres still a daunting amount left to do with guests due imminently. Ben and Billie embark on a dramatic two-bedroom apartment makeover for a Valentines Day event at Château La Grande Maison. In the Dordogne, Nick and Nicole battle to transform the façade of their dilapidated chateau into an architectural statement

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Jon returns to his favourite sport of padel, which feels like a milestone for him, as the last time he was on a court he broke his leg. But with the garden wall still not finished and Jasmine getting called away for a job, all the work is left to Jon

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton takes charge of some of the worlds most iconic locomotives. Here he visits Switzerland, travelling up front on the Glacier Express through some of Europes most beautiful scenery. Then Paul helps stoke a 1920s stream train through the mountains to a world-famous glacier. After driving a funicular train up to the Reichenbach Falls, where Sherlock Holmes met his fictional demise, he finishes by taking a train through the ice to the summit of the Eiger

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The contestants get active as Armando Iannucci adopts a new persona to perform a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani grapples with bowling pins while wearing oven gloves and Joanna Page attempts a tricky skill she hasnt tried since she was about 12. Meanwhile, a topless Joel Dommett twirls a pretty umbrella and Amy Gledhill has to cope with someone shouting about scrambled eggs. Alex Horne keeps an eye on their progress, while Taskmaster Greg Davies hands out the points - and the put-downs when it all goes wrong

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan and Lydia West. After not seeing each other for a year, Maggie and Eddie are reunited as bridesmaids at a mutual friends wedding. Maggie is dead set on showing everyone how stable she is, only to be given a junior bridesmaid status by the scary maid of honour. Eddie, meanwhile, is intent on cutting ties with Maggie for good. But first, she must team up with her estranged friend to save the wedding from a very unwelcome guest

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Maggie and Eddie discover their favourite drag act are performing for one night only - and tickets are like gold dust. Thus begins an odyssey across London to get tickets, involving wigs, theatrical icons, lies and a whole lot of drama. Maggie is desperate to remind Eddie how much fun they used to have, but Eddies new friend Whitney is determined to keep Eddie focused on getting a job to fund their future plans. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan, Lydia West and Hannah Onslow, with a guest appearance from Rupert Everett

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans brings his entertainment show back to Channel 4 in a more intimate format, featuring interviews with the biggest stars and music by the latest bands from the presenters studio at Virgin Radio UK. Tonight he is joined by Sam Ryder, Peter Capaldi and Gemma Arterton

programme.title: Sky Coppers
programme.desc: The team join the search for a woman who has left a suicide note and vanished into the night. And officers are shocked when they arrive at a house to discover a gang cruelly breeding dangerous dogs in horrific conditions

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, so the East Anglian Air Ambulance crew must make a tough decision. In Canvey Island, the medics take over the breathing of motorcyclist Sol who is bleeding from his ear. In Luton, Essex and Herts Air Ambulance help 16-year-old Idris who has a head and leg injury after a collision with a car. In Norwich, Martin has fallen from a ladder and has a nasty open ankle fracture

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including the Mixing Bowl, the Olde Stone Mill, Peters and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Alison Hammond presents the show giving people the chance to perform the one song that tells their story - often in front of a crowd for the very first time. Mentors Paloma Faith and Sam Ryder watch and listen to the performances. In the first episode, Alison heads to Liverpool, where a teenager sings Bocelli for the foster mum he cant find words to thank, and a kidney transplant recipient brings Sam and Paloma sprinting out into the downpour. With only one place available at the grand final at Londons Hackney Empire, Sam and Paloma face an impossible choice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets Andi and Neil from York, who dream of retiring permanently to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Theyre looking for a three-bedroom house with a dog-friendly garden, where barbeque enthusiast Neil can cook up a storm close to the beach close to the coastal town of Mazarron, near Murcia. Laura has five sizzling options for their budget of £145,000, but will any of them turn out to be their new forever home?

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke helps unlock the potential of two houses in south London. He begins by helping NHS theatre nurse Hannah, whose Edwardian workers cottage in Thornton Heath is so small she has had to give the only decent bedroom to her two children while she sleeps in the front room. Can he provide the luxurious master bedroom she needs? George also helps a couple renovate their Streatham home, which would not be so bad if not for the previous owners love of glass chandeliers, Roman columns and marble-effect tiles

programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam invents an elaborate lie to explain to his friends the humiliating injury he suffered in an encounter with a jealous husband. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Coach falls for an unscrupulous woman who drops him when she wins the lottery, while Sam lusts after her daughter. Bette Ford and Ellen Regan guest star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam fails to make an impression on the object of his desire, and Coach faces a tough test when the woman he loves dumps him after a big-money win on the lottery

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: All Ray wants for Christmas is a little loving from his wife, but their hopes for an intimate Christmas are ruined by their familys constant interruptions

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Enlisting for an American Civil War re-enactment allows Ray to spend time with his father, until he realises they will be fighting on opposite sides. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ally has to interview her oldest living relative as part of a school project on genealogy, so Ray helps her track down Franks long-lost Aunt Sarina, who lives in Italy but turns up on the Barones doorstep

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Crane brothers confront an unscrupulous car-park attendant over a matter of principle - and two dollars. Meanwhile, Daphne leaves Frasiers home for the last time, and realises how much Eddie and Martin are going to miss her. Kelsey Grammer stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink hopes to become the new condominium president and tries to ensure he is elected by persuading an unsuspecting Martin to stand as a puppet candidate. American comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Rozs fun-loving 20-year-old cousin visits Seattle, and Frasier finds himself struggling to keep pace with her partying. Meanwhile, Martin has trouble coping without Daphne. Long-running American comedy, guest starring Zooey Deschanel (New Girl)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Bulldog plays a practical joke on Frasier, who wastes no time in plotting revenge. Meanwhile, Niles and Daphne get caught up in a battle with a doorstep trick-or-treater. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordons disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Staffordshire to meet petrolhead Martin, whos also the organiser of a local music festival

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers are greeted by the Duchess of Northumberland on a visit to Alnwick Castle, where Matt is granted special access to the records tower, which holds precious documents detailing its role in major historical events. Janice follows in the footsteps of the castles archers of old, trying her hand with a bow and arrow in the grounds, and the family meet the Dukes official piper for the ultimate lesson on the ancient Northumbrian pipes

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps best friends of 50 years Jan and Chris find their dream holiday home in Almería, Spain, that works for both their families. In fact, between them they have six children and five grandchildren - so it could be a challenge to please everyone. With a joint budget of £150,000, Laura lines up five great options, but will Jan and Chris fall in love with one enough to end up making an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In northern Italy, ambitious pair Kevin and Laura battle against the odds to finish their tricky renovations in time for the arrival of their first retreat guests. In Spain, cameras are back with BandB owners Kirsty and Simon as they try to extend their season. Meanwhile, new parents Vicky and Aurelian rise to the challenge as they open a bakery in France

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In the Loire Valley at Château La Grande Maison, Ben and Billies unfinished apartment gets their hearts racing as try to complete it for a decadent Valentines Day event. At the Château Trois Cloches, Chris and Jolyon are tackling a full façade transformation. Near Bordeaux at Château Meaume, Mark and Sandra juggle a full gîte renovation with managing a vineyard and tractor lessons

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine and the family have been living in their Spanish villa for over a year while the builders work around them, turning it into a contemporary modern home. With a large summer party planned and guests about to arrive, can they gather momentum for the final push and complete all the work in time?

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Japan is often portrayed as a modern utopia, but beneath its polished exterior, one of the worlds oldest populations is reaching a breaking point. As traditional multi-generational families break down, millions of elderly people are being left in isolation and poverty - with many turning to petty theft to survive. As Japanese prisons become retirement homes for a growing number of elderly convicts, Krishnan Guru-Murthy investigates this geriatric crime wave gripping the country

programme.title: Battle of the Brands
programme.sub-title: Cadbury vs McVities: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: Kate Quilton peers into the cookie jar to look at the rivalry between the confectioners. She takes in the development of new products, the shrinking of existing ones, and how the contest for publicity takes in everything from the cake-v-biscuit debate and drumming gorillas. Kate also looks at how McVities are reinventing the Jaffa Cake, while Cadbury are leaning into collaborations with Biscoff, Oreo and Daim to extend their Dairy Milk empire

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 50-year-old hairstylist meets a well-travelled nutritionist, a conspiracy theorist is matched with a science teacher and an opera singer serenades his date. A high-energy spin instructor meets a passionate PE teacher. Will sparks fly, or does fate have other plans?

programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The contestants get active as Armando Iannucci adopts a new persona to perform a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani grapples with bowling pins while wearing oven gloves and Joanna Page attempts a tricky skill she hasnt tried since she was about 12. Meanwhile, a topless Joel Dommett twirls a pretty umbrella and Amy Gledhill has to cope with someone shouting about scrambled eggs. Alex Horne keeps an eye on their progress, while Taskmaster Greg Davies hands out the points - and the put-downs when it all goes wrong

programme.title: Miss Peregrines Home for Peculiar Children (2016)
programme.desc: A boy investigates a series of clues left by his grandfather, and comes across a home for children with special powers, that has been concealed from the world using time travel. It falls to the boy to protect the home and its residents from a race of invisible monsters. Fantasy adventure based on the novel by Ransom Riggs, starring Eva Green, Asa Butterfield, Samuel L Jackson and Terence Stamp

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Tower View Hotel, in Blackpool, where owner Jennifer McGirr is joined by friend Diana Carnegie. Daniel Walker and George Gillespie find some unwanted guests in the bathroom and trouble arises at breakfast when some of the guests try ordering off-menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit Church Spa in East Markham in Nottinghamshire on their second evening, where proprietor Daniel Walker and family friend George Gillespie hope to wow their guests with their relaxing facilities. Steve and Lisa Tindall seem impressed, but Jennifer and Diana Carnegie question the high price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Steve and Lisa Tindall, who own the Kings Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex, where they hope their guests will fall for their boutique BandB. They get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone gets a solid nights sleep afterwards

programme.title: Sky Coppers
programme.desc: The team join the search for a woman who has left a suicide note and vanished into the night. And officers are shocked when they arrive at a house to discover a gang cruelly breeding dangerous dogs in horrific conditions

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A nurse shares her story of finding a career she loves, and doctors suspect a 93-year-old man with a low heart rate, dizziness and confusion may need a new pacemaker fitting. A construction worker whose finger has been crushed discusses the importance of male role models in his life, and a haemophiliac reflects on living with his condition

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chefs attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: Heatwave (2022)
programme.desc: An ambitious, career-driven young woman seemingly has it all employed in a dynamic job at a real estate agency. However, her life takes a turn when she realises she has fallen for her bosss wife. Secrets are revealed and dark motives linking to her past come to light as she becomes tangled in a web of malice. Thriller, starring Kat Graham, Merritt Patterson, Sebastian Roche and Cardi Wong.

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp shows how to turn trash into treasure with Mexican tin art, soap recycling and a lovable tin can dog

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, George meets some novice DIY renovators who are planning to completely transform a dilapidated Georgian-style crescent house, poorly sub-divided into four flats in the 1980s. With a budget of just £40,000, no building experience and a plan to live onsite with two young children, the stakes could not be higher for this family

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Sam getting his life back on track after several sessions with Frasier, Diane feels it is time to come clean about her relationship with the psychiatrist. American comedy, starring Ted Danson, Shelley Long and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam discovers an intimate secret concerning Dianes romance with her new boyfriend Frasier that leads him to believe she is still madly in love with him, making his ego even bigger

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff meets the woman of his dreams at the Cheers Halloween fancy dress party - but they must work out if they feel the same about each other when the masks come off. John Ratzenberger stars alongside Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to ensure Franks 65th birthday is memorable by buying him an expensive aquarium just like the one he has always admired in a local Chinese restaurant. Unfortunately, one of the fish dies, sinking the Barone household into gloom and discord

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly attends a high-school reunion with Debra, only to discover she had been friends with the in-crowd who made his nerdy teenage years a living hell. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to intercept a strongly worded letter sent by Debra, telling Marie to stop interfering in her affairs. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink is delighted when a station in another city begins relaying his radio show - until he struggles to compete with the popularity of the host he has replaced. Elsewhere, Niles, Daphne and Martins attempt to build a table does not go according to plan

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphnes birthday party sparks a series of unexpected events, and after a night of intimacy, Frasier pursues Roz to her family reunion in Wisconsin, where he pretends to be her estranged partner Roger. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles persuades Daphnes hard-drinking father to call on her mother in Seattle, hoping he can inspire them to start afresh. Meanwhile, Frasier and Roz reflect on their night together. Comedy, guest starring Brian Cox

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Daphne impulsively marry while holidaying in Reno, but on their return they realise Frasier, Martin and Daphnes mother will be distraught if they find out they missed the event. Starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across America saving failing restaurants from disaster. In the first of two visits to Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, the chef encounters Joe Nagy, who claims he has the best menu from New York to Los Angeles. However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The lads head to Birmingham, where they fall out over a bench that Henry thinks has value

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: In Thirsk, the Bakers are given a special tour of James Herriots home by his son Jim, and Matt takes to the skies for a stunning aerial view of the county by glider. Janice meets the towns yarn-bombing guerrilla knitters and visits a stunning lavender farm with Matt. Finally, the Bakers take in what Herriot dubbed the finest view in England, at Sutton Bank

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to Mayenne in France to help engineer Martin and retired gardener Mandy find their dream forever home. After holidaying in the northwest of France many times, they now want to move lock, stock and barrel, with their three motorbikes and two prize parrots! Jean shows them five great properties for their budget of £50,000; will any of them rev Martin and Mandy up enough to make an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: A return to the Algarve to catch up with Cheryl and Graham, who swapped life in Surrey to run a riverside BandB. In France, Devon family Brian and Paula are on the move after closing their popular eatery, while on the Costa del Sol, a turn in fortune may be on the cards for guesthouse owner Louisa

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Lalacelle in Normandy, Terry and Ashley race to finish their opulent 401 suite, but theres still a daunting amount left to do with guests due imminently. Ben and Billie embark on a dramatic two-bedroom apartment makeover for a Valentines Day event at Château La Grande Maison. In the Dordogne, Nick and Nicole battle to transform the façade of their dilapidated chateau into an architectural statement

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Jon returns to his favourite sport of padel, which feels like a milestone for him, as the last time he was on a court he broke his leg. But with the garden wall still not finished and Jasmine getting called away for a job, all the work is left to Jon

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton takes charge of some of the worlds most iconic locomotives. Here he visits Switzerland, travelling up front on the Glacier Express through some of Europes most beautiful scenery. Then Paul helps stoke a 1920s stream train through the mountains to a world-famous glacier. After driving a funicular train up to the Reichenbach Falls, where Sherlock Holmes met his fictional demise, he finishes by taking a train through the ice to the summit of the Eiger

As traditional multi-generational families break down, millions of elderly people are being left in isolation and poverty - with many turning to petty theft to survive. As Japanese prisons become retirement homes for a growing number of elderly convicts, Krishnan Guru-Murthy investigates this geriatric crime wave gripping the country

programme.title: Battle of the Brands
programme.sub-title: Cadbury vs McVities: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: Kate Quilton peers into the cookie jar to look at the rivalry between the confectioners. She takes in the development of new products, the shrinking of existing ones, and how the contest for publicity takes in everything from the cake-v-biscuit debate and drumming gorillas. Kate also looks at how McVities are reinventing the Jaffa Cake, while Cadbury are leaning into collaborations with Biscoff, Oreo and Daim to extend their Dairy Milk empire

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 50-year-old hairstylist meets a well-travelled nutritionist, a conspiracy theorist is matched with a science teacher and an opera singer serenades his date. A high-energy spin instructor meets a passionate PE teacher. Will sparks fly, or does fate have other plans?

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans brings his entertainment show back to Channel 4 in a more intimate format, featuring interviews with the biggest stars and music by the latest bands from the presenters studio at Virgin Radio UK. Tonight he is joined by Sam Ryder, Peter Capaldi and Gemma Arterton

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The contestants get active as Armando Iannucci adopts a new persona to perform a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani grapples with bowling pins while wearing oven gloves and Joanna Page attempts a tricky skill she hasnt tried since she was about 12. Meanwhile, a topless Joel Dommett twirls a pretty umbrella and Amy Gledhill has to cope with someone shouting about scrambled eggs. Alex Horne keeps an eye on their progress, while Taskmaster Greg Davies hands out the points - and the put-downs when it all goes wrong

programme.title: Miss Peregrines Home for Peculiar Children (2016)
programme.desc: A boy investigates a series of clues left by his grandfather, and comes across a home for children with special powers, that has been concealed from the world using time travel. It falls to the boy to protect the home and its residents from a race of invisible monsters. Fantasy adventure based on the novel by Ransom Riggs, starring Eva Green, Asa Butterfield, Samuel L Jackson and Terence Stamp

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Tower View Hotel, in Blackpool, where owner Jennifer McGirr is joined by friend Diana Carnegie. Daniel Walker and George Gillespie find some unwanted guests in the bathroom and trouble arises at breakfast when some of the guests try ordering off-menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit Church Spa in East Markham in Nottinghamshire on their second evening, where proprietor Daniel Walker and family friend George Gillespie hope to wow their guests with their relaxing facilities. Steve and Lisa Tindall seem impressed, but Jennifer and Diana Carnegie question the high price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Steve and Lisa Tindall, who own the Kings Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex, where they hope their guests will fall for their boutique BandB. They get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone gets a solid nights sleep afterwards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former breakfast TV presenter Matt Arnold welcomes the contestants to The Bridge Hotel Brandon in Suffolk, and hopes that his mantra of beautiful beds, blissful bathrooms and brilliant breakfasts shines through. The group takes part in a rowing competition in the afternoon, but delays at breakfast and a runny egg cause irritation

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley is on the mend following his rodeo injury and determined to get back behind the wheel of the RV. As the pair continue on their journey, Barney is thrilled to be celebrating his birthday with a trip to the iconic Niagara Falls. Later, Bradley and Barney take the RV on a small cargo ferry as they head to Vermont. Here, the pair have a go at ice climbing, which proves treacherous for them and the crew, before a spot of dog sledding through the frozen wilderness and an inevitable race through the forests

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: On the final leg of their journey, the pair continue through the snow to New Hampshire. Here, they meet rally-driving champion Tim ONeil who gives them a crash course in driving in the ice and snow before they take on an intense time trial. The pair travel on to Maine, where they take part in some wilderness training, before bidding farewell to the RV and heading out into the extremely choppy seas for some lobster fishing. Before heading home, the pair look back on some of their favourite moments from the trip
